content.problemStatement:
CodeChef has just finished migrating to a new judging system.

Chef would like to test the performance of the new judging system.

Chef has some code for an older task, which he knows ran in XXX milliseconds on the old judging server.

On resubmitting the code to the new judging server, it ran in YYY milliseconds.

Which judging system is faster?


Input Format

The only line of input will contain two space-separated integers XXX and YYY — the runtime on the old judging system, and the runtime on the new judging system.


Output Format
Print:



`Old`, if the older judging system is faster
`New`, if the new judging system is faster
`Same`, if they're equally fast

Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `Old`, `OlD`, `old`, `oLD` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the runtimes (in milliseconds) on the old and the new judging systems respectively.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing one of the strings:
- "Old" if the old system is faster (X < Y)
- "New" if the new system is faster (Y < X)
- "Same" if both runtimes are equal (X == Y)
The case of the letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The old system took 255 ms and the new system took 230 ms. Since 230 < 255, the new system is faster.
The old system took 1045 ms and the new system took 1309 ms. Since 1045 < 1309, the old system is faster.
Both systems took the same time (500 ms), so they are equally fast.

solution.approach:
For each test case read X and Y. Compare the two values:
- If X < Y, the old system ran faster → output "Old".
- If Y < X, the new system ran faster → output "New".
- Otherwise they are equal → output "Same".
The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
